I opened preflop with A♦Q♥ and c‑bet the dry flop (T♠ 9♦ 4♠); Elon called. The turn J♠ is a genuine scare card — it completes Q8/KQ straights and, more importantly, brings a third spade to the board. With three spades on board any opponent holding two spades has a made flush; with one spade they have only four spades. I hold no spade and currently have only Ace‑high (with a Queen blocker that reduces some Qx straight combos). Betting here would often get called by better hands (pairs, straights, two‑spade flushes) and could force me into a costly fold versus a raise. I prefer pot control and to realize equity to the river: check. Plan: call a small turn stab but fold to a sizable raise; if checked through I will re‑evaluate river for value or bluff opportunities if I improve.

Turn board Js completes the spade flush draw (third spade) and multiple straight draws (e.g., 87, Q8 for nut str8s with J-T-9 on board). My top pair tens with 6 kicker is now highly vulnerable to flushes, straights (I block Tx but weak kicker loses to better Tx/Jx), sets, and two-pairs. Minni's flop c-bet then check suggests possible missed draw, showdown value like mid pairs/9x, or trapping with monster. Betting risks folding out better equity realizations or getting raised by made hands; thin value is poor as range unlikely to pay off worse pairs/draws that checked. Checking pot-controls OOP (acting after SB check), realizes equity cheaply (~25% pot to river), avoids bloating pot with marginal hand on scary texture. Deep stacks favor seeing river decision; if river bricks, can value bet thin or check down showdown. Consistent with flop plan to control pot post-call.

I check. On T♠9♦4♠ J♠ 3♥ with A♦Q♥ I have only Ace‑high and no spade or straight. Elon called my flop c‑bet and then checked the turn — his line is consistent with a made pair (Jx, Tx, 9x, small pocket) or a missed/one‑card spade float. There are very few worse hands that will call a river bet, and many of his likely holdings beat me and will call, so a bluff or thin value bet is low EV against this passive, calling opponent. With the pot at 1,200 and comfortable stacks, pot control and realizing showdown equity is the best play — check and see the showdown rather than investing chips into a likely losing bluff.

River 3h is a low brick that doesn't complete new draws beyond the spade flush (already live) or major straight threats, but the board remains scary with flush, multiple straights (e.g., Q8, 87 for nutstr8), and two-pair/Tx combos possible. My top pair tens (Tc) with 6 kicker blocks some Tx but is vulnerable OOP to better Tx/Jx/sets/flushes that Minni might have trapped with her flop c-bet + checks. Her line (SB minraise pre, flop cbet, check turn/river) polarizes to either strong value slowplayed (flush/set) or weak showdown/missed draw (midpairs/9x/backdoors/A-high). Betting thin value risks folding out worse (which checked behind anyway) while getting raised/folded to by better; pot odds irrelevant since checked to me. Checking pot-controls OOP, induces bluffs/missed draws to showdown where I win frequently (~40-50% vs polarized range), consistent with multi-street control plan from flop/turn. No fold equity worth chasing with marginal hand deepstacked (~15BB pot, 15k stacks). Check-behind maximizes EV long-term.

Three-handed on the button with 9♦7♣ — deep stacks (139bb) and great positional leverage. This hand is playable as an open in position (straight connectivity, reasonable equity). I want to seize initiative and avoid giving the blinds a free look, so I’ll open to a standard 3x the big blind (300 total). That sizing puts pressure on the blinds, keeps the pot controllable if I face resistance, and lets me play post‑flop in position. If I face a large 3‑bet I’ll fold; I’ll proceed post‑flop with continuation bets on favorable textures and use position to realize equity.

84o is a complete trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, no high card value, terrible post-flop playability from out of position in the small blind. Minni's button raise is standard and wide-ranging, but this hand has zero equity or bluff value against it. Minni raised to 3x the big blind from the button, which is a standard but strong opening size. My hand, K3o, is quite weak and plays poorly out of position. 3-handed on the button, acting first preflop (UTG equivalent). Pocket 44 is a strong hand in this format: above average equity multiway, excellent set-mining potential, and plays well postflop in position on both opponents. Limping or calling the BB is weak and invites multiway pots without initiative. Raising takes control, builds the pot for set value, and leverages my deep stack (170bb) and position. Standard sizing is 3x the BB to 300, which is aggressive but balanced given the short-handed play and opponents' tendencies to call/raise wide from blinds based on history (Minni frequently raises/calls preflop). If reraised, can fold to a 3-bet without issue as it's not a premium. Folding is too tight early in the tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with Q6 offsuit. Elon has opened for 300 from the Button. While the price to call is 250, Q6o is a weak hand to play out of position against a button raise, and calling would likely lead to difficult spots post-flop against a larger stack. Folding is the standard and most disciplined play here to preserve my stack.

Facing a button open to 300 from the BTN while I’m in the big blind with 6♠7♠ in a 3-handed game — this is a clear defend. Calling 200 (300 total) gives ~30% pot odds and plenty of implied odds with ~14k effective stacks to realize on straights/flushes. A 3-bet OOP would be a bluff that bloats the pot and risks a 4-bet; folding is too tight versus a standard BTN opening range. Plan: call, see a flop, play aggressively when I hit draws/pairs and fold to heavy action when I miss.

Flop: J♦ T♠ 4♠ — I have 6♠7♠. This is a clear semi‑bluff: I have a strong spade flush draw (9 spade outs) plus extra pair outs (three sixes + three sevens), so roughly ~32% to improve on the turn and ~35% to hit the flush by river. Pot is 650 and opponent checked, so betting here does two things: (1) takes the pot away from many overcards/A‑high hands and small pairs that will fold, and (2) builds the pot when I hit. With big effective stacks I want meaningful fold equity but don’t want to bloat the pot unnecessarily — a ~1/2–2/3 pot bet is ideal. Plan: bet as a semi‑bluff (350). If called I still have ~35% to make the flush by river and extra outs to pair; if I face a large raise I will largely fold (re‑evaluate for pot odds vs raise size).

Flopped bottom set with 44 on Jd Ts 4s board, an extremely strong hand that crushes villain's likely leading range (JT for top two, Jx/Tx pairs, spade flush draws like KQs/AsXs, open-enders like Q9/KQ, or bluffs). Board is draw-heavy with spade flush draw and gutshots (e.g., Q9, KQ), so raising protects against draws realizing and extracts value from worse made hands. Preflop: raised from button, she called from BB, fitting a wide defending range. Deep stacks (~167bb effective vs her 134bb) allow multi-street play, and position lets me control pot post-turn. Her small 350 bet (1/3 pot) into my c-bet spot suggests weak made hand, draw, or probe. Pot-raise to 1350 (~4x her bet) builds big pot for value, charges max from calling stations like Minni (history of calling large bets multiway, e.g., Hand 12 river 3575), denies equity to draws, and sets up turn barrel or check-raise trap if she shoves. Min-raise too small, risks cheap turn cards; overbet unnecessary deep.

I have 6♠7♠ on J♦ T♠ 4♠. After my 350 semi‑bluff Elon check‑raised to 1350 I must call 1000 more (1350 total) to continue. I have a very strong draw: 9 spade outs to a flush plus 6 clean pair outs (the remaining 6s/7s) — roughly 15 distinct outs, which gives ~54% to improve to at least a pair/flush by river. The immediate pot odds for calling 1000 into a 2350 pot (i.e. 1000 to win 3350) require only ~30% equity. My drawing equity comfortably exceeds the required equity even against a fairly value‑heavy check‑raise range, and deep stacks provide solid implied odds if I hit. Raising now would bloat the pot and turn a good semi‑bluff into an unnecessarily large gamble; folding is too conservative given the math. Plan: call 1000 more (1350 total) to see the turn; if I complete the flush or pair the turn I will extract value, if I miss and face heavy continued aggression I will re‑evaluate (likely fold to extreme turn/river action unless pot odds justify continuing).

Turn: I paired my 7 (6♠7♠ on J♦ T♠ 4♠ 7♥) and still have a strong spade draw. After Elon's aggressive check‑raise on the flop then checking the turn, his range is polarized (strong made hands and missed/semi‑bluff draws). Betting now gets value from worse hands, builds the pot when I hit, and applies fold equity against overcards/marginal holdings. The pot is 3,350 — a ~1/2‑pot bet keeps the price reasonable if he raises and maximizes my equities when called. Plan: bet 1,700 chips. If he calls I go to the river with ~14 outs (~30% to improve); if he raises big I will re‑evaluate (call medium raises because of my equity, but fold to an overcommitment/all‑in if the price is unreasonable).

Bottom set remains an extremely strong hand on this Jd Ts 4s 7h board. The turn 7h is a relative brick—it doesn't complete the spade flush draw, adds minimal new straight draws (e.g., 8-9 gutshot still weak), and doesn't improve common villain hands beyond what they had on flop. Minni's line is suspicious: calling preflop raise from BB (wide range), small flop donk into raiser (weak protection/value or draw), calling my large flop raise (1000 into ~2000 pot, committed to decent equity like Jx/Tx, strong draw, or trap), and now turn donk 1700 (~1/3 pot). This double donk suggests either medium strength (top pair/good kicker, two-pair like J7/T7 unlikely given sizing), polarized with nuts (JJ/TT/JT/set), or stubborn draw/value. However, history shows Minni sticky/calling station (e.g., Hand 12 river hero-call into huge bet; multiple multi-street calls deep). Deep effective stacks (~10k) warrant raising for max value from worse made hands (KJ/QJ/AT for TPGK, flush draws turning brick, floats) while protecting against river draws. Calling risks giving free cards; flat vs donk heavy range too passive. Raising ~3x to 5000 (pot will be ~9550 if called, ~55% pot commitment for her) builds massive pot, leverages fold equity from marginal hands, and sets up river shove if she flats. Overshove unnecessary early (Hand 20/150), risks hero-fold from two-pair; undersize misses value. Position allows control. Crush time.

Pot is 10,050 and Elon has raised the turn to 5,000 — I must call 3,300 more. I hold 6♠7♠ on J♦ T♠ 4♠ 7♥: that’s second pair (7s) plus a nine-card spade flush draw. Outs: 9 spades + 3 sixes + 2 remaining sevens = 14 distinct outs. That’s ~14/47 ≈ 29.8% to improve to a flush/trips/two‑pair by the river. Calling 3,300 into a 10,050 pot (final pot 13,350) requires ~24.7% equity (3,300/13,350). My hand’s raw equity (~30%) comfortably exceeds the break‑even point, and with deep stacks I have good implied odds if I hit.

Range/read: Elon’s line (preflop raise, flop check‑raise, turn check then big raise) is polarized — strong made hands (Jx, Tx, sets, JT) and some semi‑bluffs/draws. Versus that polarized range my equity is in the ~25–35% band, so a call is +EV. A min‑raise to 8,300 would be too committal versus many value hands; folding surrenders significant equity.

Plan: Call the 3,300 more (5000 total on the turn). If I make the flush or trips on the river I will extract maximum value; if I miss and face a large river shove I will fold; if villain checks the river I’ll check back and sometimes take a thin value stab if appropriate.

Board: J♦ T♠ 4♠ 7♥ K♥ — I hold 7♠6♠ (second pair, weak 6 kicker). Elon’s line (BTN open pre, flop check‑raise, then a very large raise on the turn) is polarised and heavily weighted toward genuine value (Jx/TX, two‑pair/sets, some Kx or turned pairs) plus a few semi‑bluffs. The river K improves many hands that already beat me and removes much of my fold equity. A river bet here will seldom get called by worse hands (most weaker holdings were folded to earlier aggression) and will almost always be called or raised by hands that beat me — so betting is negative EV. With the pot at 13,350 and my stack 7,400 I prefer pot control and taking my showdown equity rather than bloating the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: check back and go to showdown; evaluate any small river bet for a call, but avoid overcommitting to bluffs or thin value bets.

River King of Hearts is a mixed card: completes some gutshots or backdoor draws but doesn't hit the spade flush draw heavily, pairs the board without a prior king, and may improve Kx hands (KQ, KJ, KT for top pair/top two now). However, bottom set remains one of the best possible hands, crushing villain's likely range of JT (two pair), Jx/Tx (top pair), Qx draws (missed straight/flush), stubborn floats, or weaker pairs. Minni's line—defending wide pre, flop donk-call large raise, turn donk-call even larger raise, now check—screams medium strength or trapping with very strong hand, but history labels her a sticky calling station (e.g., Hand 12 river 3575 call; multiple deep calls vs aggression). Deep effective stack (her 7400 vs pot 13350) and my position demand max value extraction: shoving 7400 all-in builds pot to ~20750 if called, offers her ~2.8:1 (needing ~26% equity, which my set dominates), leverages fold equity from marginal made hands while getting called by worse (TPTK, two-pair). Checking induces no further action and forfeits value from showdown holdings; smaller bet risks fold or min-raise jam. Tournament stage (20/150) allows risk with nuts disguised. Crush value time.

I hold 7♠6♠ — after the river (J♦ T♠ 4♠ 7♥ K♥) I have second pair (sevens) with a weak 6‑kicker and my spade flush missed. Pot is 20,750 and Elon bets 7,400, so calling would cost 7,400 to win 20,750: break‑even equity ≈ 7,400 / 20,750 ≈ 35.7%. Elon's line (BTN open pre, flop check‑raise, turn check then a huge raise, and now a large river bet) is highly polarized and heavily weighted toward value (JT two‑pair, sets like JJ/TT/44, Jx/Tx, plus Kx or Q9 that now beat me). Very few combos in his range are pure bluffs that my hand beats. My realistic showdown equity vs that polarized/value‑heavy range is well below the ~36% required. Calling would commit my entire stack in a +EV‑negative spot and likely cripple me if I lose. I therefore fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

I am on the Button in a 3-handed game with Ace-Deuce offsuit (A2o). In this position, any Ace is a standard opening hand to put pressure on the blinds. My stack is healthy at 89 BBs, and while Elon has been playing very aggressively, I should still look to take control of the pot when I have positional advantage. A standard 3x raise is appropriate here.

Fold. J♥3♠ is a marginal, dominated hand facing a button open to 3bb from the small blind. I’m out of position against the raiser and the big blind (who has shown willingness to 3‑bet and be aggressive), so calling 250 or making a small isolate raise (min to 500) puts me in difficult spots postflop with poor playability. With a healthy ~73BB stack I can wait for a better spot in position or a stronger hand. Preserve chips — fold here.

Facing a button raise to 3x after SB folds, Flash's range is wide including many suited connectors, Ax, broadways. J5c suited is a standard defend from BB due to excellent playability, flush potential, straight potential (e.g., 67T, 346, etc.), and great implied odds given deep effective stacks (Flash 86BB). Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks too much with a speculative hand OOP. Calling gets good pot odds (200 into 450, ~31% equity needed, and we block Jc which caps some strong hands). Will play post-flop OOP cautiously, looking to float or realize equity.

Flop hits me hard: top pair (Jacks with Jc), plus strong club flush draw (two clubs in hand, 3c on board). This is one of the best hands in my calling range OOP. Flash's button raise range is wide: Ax suited, broadways, suited connectors, some bluffs. Board is drawy (straight draws like 45x, 89T for broadway, flush draw blockers). I have excellent equity (~60-70% vs typical range). Betting for value and protection against draws/improvers. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws or allowing bluffs. Size ~2/3 pot (433) to get calls from worse (weaker pairs, floats, gutshots) while building pot with my strong hand. Effective stacks deep (Flash 86BB), good implied if he floats. Plan: bet now, c/c or barrel turn if improves or he shows weakness.

I opened pre-flop with Ace-Deuce offsuit (A2o) from the button, and Elon called from the Big Blind. The flop comes Jack-Seven-Trey rainbow, which misses my range significantly. Elon has decided to lead out (donk bet) for roughly 66% of the pot. While Elon has been playing very aggressively in previous hands, A2o has very little equity here and no real backdoor draws. Calling would likely lead to facing more pressure on the turn and river with only Ace-high. Given Elon's history of multi-street aggression, I will fold and wait for a better spot rather than trying to float or bluff on a board that favors a defending range that could easily contain a Jack or a middle pair.

3-handed, on the Button with A♦?AhTd (Ace–Ten offsuit) and ~73BB effective — this is a strong, playable hand heads-up. I want to seize initiative, build a pot with position and deny cheap flops to the blinds, so open-raising is correct. Size to 3x the big blind (raise to 300) — big enough to charge the blinds and get fold equity, yet small enough to keep postflop playability vs deep stacks. If I face a standard 3-bet I can often call in position and play the flop; fold or fold to very large 3-bets/shoves depending on sizing and reads.

Pocket nines is a premium hand facing a button raise from Minni, who has shown aggression but folded to 3-bets in the past (e.g., Hand 6). The button range is wide, including many hands I dominate. 3-betting builds the pot, applies fold equity against Minni and the BB (Flash), and isolates if called. Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is standard for value and pressure with deep stacks. Folding or calling is too passive; set-mining not ideal OOP.

I am facing a raise from the Button (Minni) and a 3-bet from the Small Blind (Elon). While A7o has some raw equity, it plays very poorly out of position in a 3-bet pot, especially when sandwiched between two active players. Elon has shown significant aggression and a large stack, making a call or a 4-bet bluff risky with this specific hand. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I opened to 300 and Elon 3-bet to 900; pot is 1300 and I need to call 600 (total committed = 900). With ~70BB effective and A♥T♦ in position, the correct, lower-variance line is to call. ATo plays very well in position against a 3-betting range — it has decent equity, keeps worse hands in, and lets me use position postflop. A small 4-bet (min to 1500) would polarize my range too much and is unlikely to fold out enough of Elon's range; a larger 4-bet unnecessarily bloats the pot out of position postflop. Plan: call to see a flop; c-bet/press when I connect or have strong draws, control pot or fold to heavy resistance on dry, unconnected boards.

Pocket 99 is an overpair on a 5d6cTs flop, which is relatively dry with potential open-end straight draws (e.g., 78) and gutshots. Minni's button calling range after my pre-flop 3-bet includes TT+, AKo/s, suited broadways, and some suited connectors, much of which I dominate or have showdown value against. As OOP, c-betting for value and protection is standard; it targets worse pairs, AxTx, draws. Sizing to ~2/3 pot (1300 into 1900) balances value extraction while denying equity to draws and keeping fold equity against floats. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs.

I have top pair (T) with the best kicker (A) on a relatively dry 5♦-6♣-T♠ flop. Elon 3‑bet preflop so his continuing range includes overpairs (QQ+/JJ), sets (less likely), and a lot of broadway/ace hands (AK/AQ) and some bluffs. His 1,300 c‑bet into ~3,200 is a standard sizing and calling is cheap — I only need ~29% equity to break even. Calling keeps worse tens and ace-x hands and bluffs in, preserves position, and gives me control to evaluate turn cards. A raise would polarize my range, bloat the pot against hands that beat me (overpairs/sets), and won’t fold enough of his continuing range to justify it. Plan: call the 1,300, then re‑assess on the turn (value raise if I improve or exploit weakness, pot‑control or fold to heavy resistance if the board gets dangerous).

Pocket nines is still a strong overpair on this 5d6cTs3c board. The turn 3c brings a club flush draw (matching the 6c), potentially strengthening some suited club calling hands from the flop, but her range for calling the pre-flop 3-bet and flop c-bet IP includes TT+, AQ+, suited broadways, and speculative suited connectors/gappers like 78s, 47s, J7s (club versions stronger now). However, 99 blocks sets, remains ahead of most pairs (77-88, TxAx), busted draws, and floats. No obvious straight completions (e.g., 47 was gutshot). With pot 4500 and Minni's stack at 5150 (SPR ~1.1), betting extracts value from worse hands/draws while denying equity to flush draws or any remaining straight draws. Sizing to 3000 (~2/3 pot) builds a pot larger than her stack, applying max pressure; she can call off with better or hero-call worse, but fold equity vs marginal hands maximizes EV. Checking risks giving a free river card to draws or allowing her to bluff/value bet IP.

I have top pair with the best kicker (A♦T♥) on a dry 5♦-6♣-T♠-3♣ board. Elon has been aggressive (3-bet pre, c-bet on the flop, now a sizeable turn bet), but the price to call 3,000 into a 7,500 pot requires only ~28.6% equity (3,000 / 10,500). ATo has decent equity versus a realistic continuing 3-bet range (overpairs/sets plus many Ax and broadway bluffs), and calling keeps worse tens and ace-x bluffs in while controlling pot size. A raise would polarize my range and commit most of my stack against hands that currently beat me (QQ+/sets); folding is too passive since I beat a lot of his range. I will call the turn, then reevaluate on the river — extract value on safe rivers, fold to very large river aggression if the board or action strongly indicates a superior hand.

Pocket 99 on 5d6cTs3cAd river. No flush or straight completed (club FD bricked, diamond only two, no str8 possible). 99 beats lower pairs (88-J9 no A/T), Tx without A, busted draws; loses to Ax (top pair), AT (two pair AT), TT (trips), QQ+/KK/AA (overpairs), unlikely AJ+ lower. Minni's range (button call OOP to SB 3bet pre, +flop/turn calls totaling committed 5150 stack) is strong: TT+, AQ+/AJs+, suited broadways/connectors (78s,47s for draws). Ace r improves Ax heavily but bricks club/straight draws. OOP first to act, pot 10500, her 2150 left (SPR<1). Value betting thin: low fold equity vs committed range (she calls Ax+/draws), gets called by better. Bluffing unnecessary with SDV. Check optimal: induces bluffs from bricked draws (history shows Minni folds marginal rivers e.g. H7,H20), extracts value/value-bets Ax (call shove 2150 into 12k+ needs ~14% eq, 99 has 15-25% vs pol range), checks back worse. Maximizes EV long-term.

I have A♥T♦ on 5♦-6♣-T♠-3♣-A♦ — two pair, Aces and Tens. Elon 3‑bet pre, led the flop and barreled the turn, then checked the river. His river-check line includes a lot of Ax and broadway-type hands (AK/AQ/KQ/etc.) and some overpairs/medium pairs; the hands that still beat me (AA, TT, or the rare set/trips) are combo‑limited. The pot is huge (10,500) and my remaining stack is 2,150 — an all‑in jam is only ~20% of the pot. Betting my stack here is a thin value shove: it extracts from worse A‑x and single pair hands that will call a small bet, charges hands that might consider calling (Kx, Qx, missed broadways), and sets a clear decision for Elon (the few combos that beat me will call anyway). Checking is too passive and leaves value on the table; raising earlier would have been polarizing. I will shove my remaining 2,150 to maximize value on a river that safely improved my hand.

Pocket 99 on 5d6cTs3cAd river remains a strong overpair with significant showdown value against Minni's polarized range after calling preflop 3-bet OOP, flop c-bet, and turn bet (now committed). The Ad bricks club flush draws and most straight draws (no completed straights possible), improving her Ax hands to top pair but also capping her value range at AT+, TT trips, QQ+/KK/AA overpairs (unlikely given action). She likely bluffs with busted club flush draws (e.g., AcXc, suited connectors like 74s/87s that missed), Ax high (weak kicker), or lower pairs fearing my check strength. Pot odds are excellent: 2150 to call into 12650+2150=14800 (~12.7% equity needed). 99 has ~20-30% equity vs her value range (beats worse Ax/Tx pairs, draws) and 100% vs bluffs. Her history shows river aggression (H12 raise, H7 check-raise but folded turn), but also folds to pressure (H20); with SPR <1 on turn call, her shove mixes value/bluffs. Calling maximizes EV OOP, extracts from bluffs, holds vs value; folding too tight, raising impossible (all-in). Builds stack long-term vs exploitable aggression.

3-handed on the button with As9h after SB folds. A9o is a strong raising hand in this spot: dominated only by better aces, blocks AA/AK, live 9 kicker, and position. BB defends wide 3-handed, but we have good equity and fold equity. Limping is suboptimal; raising isolates and builds pot OOP for opponent. Standard sizing 3x BB to 300 total. Deep stacks support this.

I am in the Small Blind with 2c 3s, which is one of the weakest possible starting hands in Texas Hold'em. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. Calling 250 more to play this hand out of position against a deep-stacked, aggressive opponent is a losing play. There is no strategic reason to defend this hand; folding is the standard and most profitable decision to preserve my stack.

I’m in the big blind with K♠Q♥ facing a 300 open from the button in a 3-handed game. This is a strong hand heads-up and Button's open is likely wide. I want to take the initiative, isolate the opener, and get value while denying equity to speculative hands. I’ll 3-bet to 900 (about 3x the open): large enough to build the pot and generate fold equity, but leaves stacks deep for postflop play. If Elon 4-bets very large or jams I’ll re-evaluate and likely fold KQo to a shove; if he calls I’ll continue with aggression on favorable flops.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni in the BB after my BTN open-raise with As9o. This is a strong hand with position heads-up: ace blocker, decent equity vs wide 3-bet ranges (88+, AJs+, AQo+, some bluffs), live kicker. Pot odds are excellent at 600 to win 1250 (~3:1, need 24% equity but have ~35-40%). Minni is aggressive historically (frequent PF raises, continues betting), but deep stacks favor realizing equity in position rather than folding or 4-betting a non-premium. Calling keeps pot manageable OOP for her, allows post-flop control with initiative. Will reassess on flop.

I have K♠Q♥ on a 3♣ J♠ Q♠ flop — top pair with the best kicker and a single spade blocker (K♠). The board is somewhat wet (two spades plus straight combos like T9), so I want to charge draws and get value from worse Queens, Jx and Kx. Because I was the preflop 3-bettor, a continuation bet is very natural and keeps the initiative. I’ll size about half‑pot to balance value and pot control while leaving room to maneuver on the turn. If Elon check‑raises large (polarized), I’ll strongly consider folding to a shove or re-evaluating against a pot‑sized raise; if he makes a small raise I’ll call and continue. Bet ~½ pot (900) to get value and deny equity to draws.

Nut flush draw (As) on QsJs3c board, position heads-up vs Minni's donk bet of 900 into 1850 pot (now 2750, ~33% pot bet). Excellent pot odds (900 to win 3650, ~20% needed, have 35-45% vs her 3-bet range of QQ+,AK,AJ+,bluffs/Axs). Her BB 3-bet then flop donk suggests polarized: premiums (Qx,Jx,sets), draws (KTs,ATs,T9s), or probes. History shows Minni aggressive but folds to heavy aggression (Hand 20 shove, Hand 12 river). Raising builds pot with strong draw, charges worse draws/pairs, denies equity/free cards, high fold equity from bluffs. Call too passive with position/implied odds (SPR ~4 post-call); fold absurd. Size ~2.5x her bet to 2250 total: balanced, commits ~13% stack, pot becomes ~5000 if called. Reassess turn with position.

Elon check‑raised my 900 c‑bet to 2250 on 3♣ J♠ Q♠. I have K♠Q♥ — top pair with the best kicker and a spade blocker that removes some strong spade+Kx combos; my Q also removes some Qx two‑pair combos. Elon’s raise is large and polarized: it contains heavy value (QJ, sets, some two‑pairs/overpairs) but also plausible semi‑bluffs (two‑spade combos, T9/Tx straights). Given stack depths and that I was the preflop 3‑bettor, I prefer to keep worse queens and Jx in his range and avoid committing more with just one pair. Raising would overcommit into a polarized range and isn’t necessary to extract value here. I’ll call the 1350 more (2250 total on the flop), realize equity, and re‑evaluate on the turn — continue for value if villain shows weakness or I improve, fold to extreme aggression if the turn completes obvious stronger hands.

I hold K♠Q♥ and the turn Q♦ gives me trip queens with a K kicker. Elon was the preflop opener and check‑raised my flop c‑bet very large, so his range contains strong value (sets JJ/33, Qx that turn into trips/full houses, maybe two‑pairs) as well as bluffs/semi‑bluffs (spade and straight draws). The turn improves me to a very strong but not invulnerable hand — pocket J’s or 3’s that check‑raised the flop now become full houses and would beat me. That said, many of Elon’s hands (Jx, Kx, missed draws) will call a value bet. I should extract value and deny free cards rather than slow‑play: bet a size that gets called by worse pairs and missed draws but doesn’t automatically fold out everything. I’ll bet ~4,000 (~60% pot). If Elon reacts with a large polarized raise or shove I’ll strongly suspect a full house or superior trips and fold to extreme overbets; I will call smaller raises and reevaluate on the river.

Turn pairs the Queens on a QsJs3c Qd board, completing possible straights (T9) but more importantly giving boats or trips to Qx hands. Minni's line (BB 3-bet pre, flop donk into draw-heavy board, call my raise, turn donk lead) is very strong, likely QQ (now boat), Qx (trips/boat), or polarized with bluffs/missed spades draws. My As9h nut flush draw still live (~10 clean spade outs for ~40% equity vs QQ/value range, higher vs air). Pot odds to call 4000 into 14350 (~22% needed) are great, but SPR post-call ~1.7 effective (her 3650 behind), committing us to river jam/check-jam. Raising denies her equity, applies max fold equity to marginal made hands (AJs-QJs for two pair, missed draws, probes), extracts value from worse (KQ/AQ/JQ two pair), and gets all-in now with ~40%+ equity vs calling range. Her history shows aggression but folds to pressure (e.g., Hand 20 shove fold). Position irrelevant with jam; dead money from her lead justifies overbet shove. Folding draw absurd with equity/Pot odds.

I have K♠Q♥ — trips (Q♦ on turn gives me Qh+Qs+Qd) with a K kicker. Pot is 23,900 and Elon shoved to 13,550 leaving me 7,650 to call (calling would be my last chips). That call requires ~7,650/31,550 ≈ 24.3% equity to break even. 

Line reading: Elon opened, called my 3-bet, then check‑raised my flop C‑bet very large and now shoves the turn. That sequence is highly polarized and heavily weighted to value on this runout. Hands that now beat my trips include pocket Jacks (only 3 combos after Js on board), pocket Threes (3 combos), and Qx hands that include a Jack or a Three (Qc+J or Qc+3 — a few combos) — roughly a dozen clear full‑house combos. While there are some plausible bluffs/semi‑bluffs (two‑spade combos, straight draws) in his flop check‑raise range, the turn shove size and prior aggression make it likely his shove is value‑heavy. 

I previously planned to fold to an extreme polarized shove, and the current raise is exactly that. Given the high proportion of his range that now contains full houses (which have essentially 0% equity vs my trips) and the fact I must commit my entire stack, calling is negative EV. I fold and preserve my 7,650 chips for better spots rather than risking them into a very narrow value range that dominates me.

I am on the button with K4o. In a 3-handed game, this is a marginal opening hand. However, Elon (the chip leader) has been extremely aggressive recently, winning several large pots and showing a willingness to 3-bet and push players off hands. Minni is also likely playing cautiously or looking for a spot to double up after a major loss in the previous hand. Given the stack dynamics and Elon's momentum, I'll fold this weak King and wait for a more favorable situation to engage.

I have 4♦8♥ in the small blind. This is a weak, unsuited hand and I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position against Elon’s very deep stack, so I’ll just take a cheap flop. Calling 50 more (100 total committed) gives ~25% pot odds to see the flop and preserves stack flexibility. Postflop I’ll play very cautiously: fold to large aggression on dry boards, continue with any pair or strong draw, and look to keep the pot small unless I improve. If Elon shows preflop strength (a reraise) I will let it go.

Preflop in BB facing a SB limp after BTN fold. My hand is 6c7h, an off-suit connector with some playability but not strong enough to raise out of position heads-up against Minni, who has shown aggression in prior hands. Pot is 200, I'm getting a free flop with decent implied odds if I connect, and stacks are deep enough (238BB effective with Minni's 75BB). Checking keeps the pot small and allows me to realize equity postflop without committing chips OOP on a marginal hand. Raising risks isolation with a speculative hand and potential domination.

I completely missed this flop with 4♦8♥: no pair and no meaningful draw (flush is impossible with only one heart on board and one in my hand). Elon in the big blind is deep and has shown a wide yet aggressive range in prior hands, so a bet here would be a thin bluff that’s likely to be called or raised by many hands that beat me (Ax, Kx, pocket pairs). Pot control is priority — check to keep the pot small and preserve stack. If Elon bets I will fold to significant pressure; if he checks back I can consider a delayed bluff on a safe turn or a small stab on cards that help my story, but for now I won’t invest chips with no equity.

Flop board Ks As 9h is very dry but high-card heavy, favoring strong ace or king ranges from Minni's SB limp-call, which can be wide but includes Ax, Kx, pairs. My 6c7h has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely), no flush draw. Preflop check was correct for implied odds, but flop whiffed completely. With action checked to me OOP, betting is a pure bluff into a limper who may have hit and check to trap or induce. Checking maintains pot control, realizes any thin fold equity later if needed, and avoids bloating pot with air when facing potential check-raise. Effective stacks 75BB allow multi-street play, but no reason to bluff now on uncoordinated board. Plan to check turn/river unless massive improvement.

I have 4♦8♥ and have completely missed this board. The turn (K♠ A♠ 9♥ 2♠) is ugly — three spades on board and I hold no spade, no pair and no realistic draw. Elon checked both flop and turn, showing pot-control or a value hand and he’s very deep, so my fold equity is low and a bet here would be a thin bluff likely to be called or raised by better hands (A/K/paired hands). Priority is pot control and preserving stack depth for better spots. I’ll check and see the river for a free card; if Elon fires a bet on the turn I’ll fold to significant pressure given my weak holding and stack dynamics. I can consider a river bluff only if a very favorable card arrives and the situation gives a credible story.

Turn board Ks As 9h 2s completes spades flush draw and adds straight possibilities (e.g., QT for broadway). My 6c7h remains complete air: no pair, no draws, zero showdown value. Minni's SB limp-check range is wide but includes Ax, Kx, sets, flush draws that hit this board heavily. She checked flop and turn OOP, potentially weak (missed draws) or trapping strong hands. Pot is tiny at 200 with deep effective stacks (75BB Minni), but I'm OOP heads-up. Betting is a poor bluff spot: high fold equity unlikely vs. limper's capping range, risks check-raise from monsters, bloats pot unnecessarily. Checking controls pot, induces her river bluffs/value bets I can hero-call if needed (unlikely), or check down to showdown cheaply. Consistent with preflop/flop plan of playing speculative hand postflop passively when missing. Will evaluate river for potential bluff if she checks again and board worsens, but check now preserves stack and realizes any fold equity passively.

I have 4♦8♥ — complete air on a King-Ace-9-2-3 rainbow board. I missed every street (no pair, no meaningful draw) and Elon checked flop and turn, showing pot control or a made hand. With a very deep and occasionally sticky opponent, my fold equity on a river bluff is minimal and a bet is a thin bluff that’s likely to be called or raised by A/K/x and pairs that beat me. The pot is small (200) relative to stacks, so there’s no reason to risk chips for a low-probability bluff. Best play is to take the free showdown: check and preserve the stack for better spots.

River board Ks As 9h 2s 3d is draw-heavy (spades flush completed, possible straight draws like QJ or T10 missed but board pairs unlikely). My 67o is pure air: no pair, no completed draw, zero showdown value against Minni's SB limp-check range, which caps weak (missed draws, low pairs) but includes Ax/Kx/spades that hit hard. She's checked OOP all streets, potentially trapping monsters or weak hands planning to hero. Pot tiny at 200, effective stack 75BB (her 7550). Betting OOP is disastrous: low fold equity vs. sticky limper (history shows calling stations like Hand #1, #2), risks check-raise from value, commits unnecessary chips with nothing. Checking realizes any equity passively (she might bluff), induces her bluffs/value thin I snap-fold to cheaply, preserves deep stack for better spots in tournament (Hand 24/150, goal max stack). Consistent passive postflop plan with speculative hand OOP after whiffing. Check behind to showdown or face her river action.

I’m on the button with Kc-Qd — a strong, playable hand in a 3-handed pot. Limping (call 100) surrenders initiative and invites multiway action; raising gains fold equity and lets me play postflop in position. Standard, balanced sizing here is ~3x the big blind — I’ll raise to 300 total: big enough to price out many speculative hands and discourage light steals, but small enough to keep pot control and room to maneuver if Elon (SB) 3-bets. Plan: call a small/standard 3-bet and play in position; fold to an over-aggressive shove out of line with the size.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call after my blind). My hand is 6s5h offsuit, which is a speculative connector but very weak, especially out of position postflop. Minni raises frequently from the button based on history, but 65o has poor playability multiway or heads-up OOP against a raiser. Effective stacks are deep (23k/7.5k), but no fold equity or strong enough equity to justify calling. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with 7-3 offsuit, which is one of the weakest possible starting hands. Minni raised from the Button to 300. Although Minni's range is likely wide in this position, 7-3 offsuit has very poor playability and I will be out of position for the remainder of the hand. With a healthy stack of approximately 83 big blinds, there is no strategic reason to defend with such a marginal hand. Folding is the standard and most profitable play in this situation.

3-handed on button preflop with pot 150 (blinds posted, likely SB folded and BB checked given no actions listed and to-call 100). Hand 5d8h offsuit is trash: poor equity, bad playability, reverse implied odds if called or check-raised. Limping creates multiway pots with weak hand, bad. Raising risks deep stack vs shorter opponents who defend wide based on history of frequent check-calls and showdowns. Standard play is to fold weak hands like this and wait for better spots to steal or value bet. Preserves stack for +EV opportunities.

I have a strong starting hand (KQo) in the Small Blind. In a three-handed game, this is well above the average hand strength. Since Elon folded on the button, I am only facing Minni in the Big Blind. I will raise to take the initiative and build a pot with a premium broadway hand. A standard raise to 300 (3x the big blind) is appropriate here to put pressure on Minni and potentially win the pot pre-flop or build it for post-flop play.

Flash opened to 300 from the small blind, making the pot 400 and my call 200. Calling would require ~33% equity (200/(400+200)=33%), but J♦2♣ offsuit is a very weak, often-dominated hand and plays terribly out of position. With ~78 BB effective there are plenty of better spots to invest chips and I don't want to play a marginal hand OOP against a likely wide-but-strong SB opening. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better situation (I would consider a 3-bet bluff with a hand that has more blockers or post-flop playability, but not J2o).

I am holding Ace-King offsuit on the button in a three-handed game, which is a premium hand. My goal is to raise for value and take control of the hand. Given the stack sizes and the aggressiveness of Elon and Minni in previous hands, a standard raise to 3x the big blind is appropriate to build the pot and potentially thin the field or win the blinds immediately.

I have 6s4d in the small blind facing a 3x button open to 300. This is a weak, low offsuit hand that's often dominated and plays terribly out of position. To call I must put in 250 into a 450 pot, which requires >35.7% equity to break even preflop (250 / (450+250)) — optimistic once the big blind still has to act and can call or reraise. A 3-bet bluff to 500 would be very thin here and could get punished by either opponent. I’ll fold and preserve my ~77bb stack for better spots.

Qh5d is a marginal offsuit hand with little connectivity, suitedness, or blocking value. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has shown aggression in recent hands (e.g., Hand 21, 26), their range is wide but includes many better hands. Pot odds are ~2.25:1 (200 to call into 450), but post-flop playability is poor: dominated often, few draws, hard to realize equity. Defending wide in BB vs button is standard, but Q5o is bottom-tier and not worth the risk with deep stacks (mine 23k, effective ~8k). Folding preserves stack for superior spots in this tournament phase (hand 27/150). No strong reason to 3-bet bluff here given hand strength.

Fold. 6♥3♠ offsuit is a very weak, easily dominated hand with almost no showdown value. There’s already a raise (to 200) and at least one caller, so this will be a multiway pot and my equity is poor. Calling 100 or trying to 3-bet as a bluff is low EV against two deeper stacks — better to conserve chips on the button and wait for a better spot or a clear steal opportunity.

Pocket Aces is the best starting hand in poker. Button (Minni) has folded, leaving us heads-up against the Big Blind (Flash) who will defend a wide range from BB. With deep effective stacks (opponents around 77-85BB), we raise to a standard 3x BB size to build the pot, deny equity to weaker hands, and isolate if called. Raising to 300 is aggressive but balanced, representing a strong range while getting value from BB's calling range. Folding or limping would be a massive mistake with AA.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Seven offsuit. Elon, the chip leader in the Small Blind, has raised to 300. I have position on Elon for the rest of the hand. Given the pot odds (200 to call into a 400 pot) and the fact that I am in position against a likely wide Small Blind opening range, calling is the most standard and profitable play with this hand.

Pocket Aces remain extremely strong on this flop of 2s-2c-Qd, pairing the board with an overpair to the Queen. Flash's BB defending range includes Qx, overpairs (KK-JJ, smaller pairs), suited broadways, and some speculative hands, many of which we dominate. No strong draws exist (rainbow-ish board), reducing fold equity needs. As OOP aggressor preflop, a standard c-bet extracts value from worse hands like Qx, mid-pairs, and floats while building the pot with our nuts-equivalent equity. Betting 2/3 pot (400) balances value and protection against any villainous turn cards. Larger bet risks folding out too much; check invites free cards to overcards/draws.

On a board of 2s-2c-Qd, Elon's continuation bet of 400 into a 600 pot is a standard aggressive move from the Small Blind. Although I have King-high, which might be ahead of some of his total bluffs, this board texture hits a lot of a standard opening range (any Queen or pocket pair). Without a pair or a draw, calling 400 with just King-high out of position is a losing play in the long run, especially against an aggressive chip leader who is likely to barrel on the turn. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

3-handed on the button first to act pre-flop with T6o (offsuit, mediocre high card, poor connectivity and playability). While button opening range is wide (~50%+ in theory), T6o is marginal at best: low equity vs calling ranges, limited straight potential, no suited/flush value. Deep stacks (240BB effective), but opponents have shown loose pre-flop raising tendencies (Minni frequently raises to 200-300). Folding avoids tough post-flop spots OOP if they 3-bet or call wide. Preserve stack for premium spots; EV+ to wait for better openings to maximize long-term stack growth in 150-hand tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with J4 suited. With the Button folding, it is a blind-vs-blind situation against Minni. J4s is a reasonable hand to play in this spot. While limping is an option, raising allows me to take control of the hand and potentially win the pot immediately. Given the stack depths, a standard raise to 300 is appropriate to test the Big Blind's defense.

Qs6♦ is a marginal hand but worth defending the big blind here. Flash opened to 300 from the small blind in short-handed play — likely a fairly wide raising range — and calling is cheap with stacks ~75–80bb. I don’t want to 3-bet: Q6o has a weak kicker and will often be dominated, and a 3-bet would bloat a pot out of position. So I’ll call to see a flop, with a plan to fold to heavy aggression if I miss and to continue only with top pair/strong draws or good turn improvements. (Call 200 more; total committed on this street = 300.)

I have flopped middle pair (Jacks) with a Four kicker on an Ace-high board. While I was the pre-flop aggressor, betting into an Ace-high board with second pair and a weak kicker often results in getting called by better hands (Aces or Jacks with better kickers) and folding out worse hands. Checking allows me to control the size of the pot and evaluate Minni's response. It also protects my checking range, as I can have some Aces or Jacks that check here to induce bluffs or see a cheap turn.

Flop is Js 2s Ah; I hold Qs6d — no pair, just Q-high with a weak backdoor spade possibility (needs both turn+river). Flash opened from the small blind pre and then checked the flop; in heads-up I have position. This is a classic pot‑control / information spot: my hand is marginal and dominated by much of his checking range (Ax, Jx, better Qx), so barreling as a bluff is thin and bloats the pot with little equity. I’ll check to see the turn, plan to fold to meaningful aggression, and only continue if I improve (pair a Q or pick up a spade) or if he gives a cheap price to call. Checking preserves stack and keeps options in position.

I have middle pair (Jacks) with a weak kicker on an Ace-high board. After checking the flop and seeing Minni check back, the turn 8 of clubs is relatively neutral. Since I am out of position and my hand has moderate showdown value but is vulnerable to any Ace or a better Jack, checking again is the most prudent play. This allows me to keep the pot small, potentially induce a bluff from a missed draw, or see a cheap river card. If Minni bets, I will re-evaluate based on her sizing, but for now, pot control is the priority.

Board is Js-2s-Ah-8c and I hold Qs6d — no pair and no realistic draw (the backdoor spade flush is dead after the club turn). Flash was the preflop raiser and then checked the flop and turn, a line consistent with Ax, some Jx, medium pairs or a missed broadway; my Q6 is largely dominated. Pot is 600 with stacks ~7.5k (SPR ≈12), so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. I’ll check to control the pot size, gather information, and reserve folding equity — folding to significant turn aggression is fine. I’ll only continue if I improve on the river (pair a Q/6) or if Flash gives a very cheap price to see showdown or shows weakness on the river that I can credibly bluff-catch or bluff against.

I have middle pair (Jacks) on an Ace-high board with a weak kicker. The hand has been checked down on both the flop and the turn, suggesting that Minni likely does not have a strong Ace or a set. My hand has significant showdown value in a pot this size. Betting now is unlikely to get called by worse hands, as most hands I beat will simply fold, and I might get called or raised by a better Jack or a slow-played Ace. Checking is the most reliable way to reach showdown and win the pot against her range of weak pairs or missed draws.

I will check. Holding Q♠6♦ I missed this board (J♠ 2♠ A♥ 8♣ 5♦) — no pair and no realistic draw. Flash was the preflop raiser but has checked flop, turn and river, a passive line consistent with A‑x, J‑x, small pairs or complete air. My hand is largely dominated and has very little showdown value, so bluffing is thin and would bloat the pot unnecessarily. Checking controls the pot and preserves stack; I’ll fold to any meaningful bet and would only call a very small probe if the price is tiny.

I am on the button with 82 suited. While 3-handed play allows for a wider opening range from the button, 82s is near the bottom of that range. Elon, the big blind, is the table chip leader and has shown significant aggression in previous hands (e.g., Hand #20 and Hand #23). Opening with a weak hand like 82s into a large, aggressive stack is risky as I'll likely face a 3-bet or be forced into difficult post-flop decisions out of position. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Three-handed the small blind should open a wide range. 9♦3♦ is marginal but suited and playable heads-up, so I want to take the initiative and try to steal the BB rather than limp and give Elon free equity. I’ll raise to the standard 2x (200) to generate fold equity and define his range. If Elon jams or 3-bets to a large size (well above ~3x my raise) I’ll fold — this hand doesn’t play well facing heavy preflop aggression OOP. If he just calls I’ll proceed cautiously postflop, looking to realize equity (flush/paired board) and c-bet small on favorable flops, check back dry/unfavorable boards to control the pot.

Preflop in BB facing a standard min-raise to 200 from SB after BTN fold. Pot is 300, calling 100 more for 25% pot odds. Hand is 5h6c, suited connectors with good playability and implied odds given deep stacks (mine 23.8k, hers 7.25k). Minni raises frequently from early positions per history, suggesting a wide range including steals from SB. Defending wide OOP vs SB is standard. Folding too tight, 3-betting not suitable for this hand. Calling to realize equity multiway potential or hit flops hard.

Flop (Jh Ks 5c) completely misses 9♦3♦ — no diamond, no pair, only a very small backdoor chance. Elon called preflop from the big blind so his defending range is wide and includes many hands that will either call a c-bet or continue — with deep stacks he’s willing to turn aggression into a big pot. This is a dry, unfavorable board for a continuation bet: low equity, poor showdown value, out of position. I’ll check to control the pot, realize equity cheaply, and re-evaluate on the turn; fold to large aggression unless I pick up real equity or a good bluffing opportunity.

Flop board J♥ K♠ 5♣ is somewhat coordinated with possible straight draws (e.g., Q-T, A-Q), but rainbow suits limit flush draws. I have bottom pair (5s) with a mediocre 6 kicker and no strong draw (gutshot to 7 unlikely to be clean). Preflop, defended suited connector 5h6c wide vs. SB min-raise for playability and implied odds. Minni checked OOP, representing weakness or trap, but her aggressive history suggests wide preflop range (Ax, Kx, suited broadways/connectors). Pot 400, OOP, pot control is key with marginal made hand. Betting risks folding out better equity or facing raise; checking keeps pot small, realizes equity on turns, controls pot size given deep stacks (effective ~7k). Plan to bet stronger turns or check-fold weak ones.

I have 9♦3♦ on Jh Ks 5c Qs — no pair, no diamond, no meaningful draw. I checked the flop to control the pot and realize equity; the turn Q further improves many hands in Elon’s defending range (Qx, Kx, Jx, broadway combinations). Betting now would be a pure bluff out of position into a deep stack and a relatively small pot; it risks a raise or call from hands that beat me and commits too many chips with virtually no fold equity against a player who has shown willingness to call/raise. Pot control is the priority — I’ll check and see the river, then decide whether to bluff or fold based on the card and Elon’s action.

Turn board brings Q♠, completing Broadway straight draws (A-T) and adding flush draw potential (spades). My bottom pair 5s with 6 kicker is very weak against villain's SB opening range, which hits Kx, Qx, Jx, Ax, suited connectors hard. She checked flop and turn OOP, showing reluctance to bet, possibly weak or trapping, but pot control is crucial with marginal hand on dangerous board. Deep effective stacks (~7k), but no strong draws or equity to justify betting; betting risks folding out worse or getting raised/folded on by better. Checking realizes equity cheaply, controls pot, allows free river card or fold to bet. Preflop speculative defend doesn't warrant thin value here.

I hold 9d3d on Jh Ks 5c Qs Jc — no pair, no flush, no meaningful blocker. I checked flop and turn to control the pot and realize equity; the river pairing the J makes many hands in Elon's checked range (Jx, Kx, Qx, pocket pairs) either beat me or will call a bluff. Elon has been passive this hand, so my fold equity is very small and a bluff is low EV. I will check and go to showdown; if Elon bets I will fold to meaningful pressure to protect my stack.

River pairs the jack (Jh and Jc on board), making the board very coordinated: possible trips (any Jx), two pair (Kx, Qx with board Js), broadway straights (A-T), or ace-high. My bottom pair (5s, 6 kicker) has minimal showdown value against Minni's SB min-raise range, which includes Ax, Kx, Qx, Jx, suited broadways/connectors that connect well here. She checked all streets OOP, indicating weakness or missed draws rather than trapping strength, given her aggressive history when strong (e.g., Hands #7,20,23). Pot is small (400) with deep effective stacks (~7k), so no need to risk betting thin value into a calling station or face raises from better (Jx trips, Kx/Qx two pair). Checking realizes equity at showdown cheaply, wins vs pure air/A-high bluffs/missed draws (plausible for SB minraise), and controls pot size. Betting risks folding out worse hands while getting called/raised by better; fold equity insufficient for bluff given checked-to spot. Consistent with prior streets' pot control strategy.

Three-handed on the button with 4♣5♦. Pot is 150 and it only costs 100 to see the flop — cheap given my position and decent postflop playability (wheel/straight and occasional two-pair possibilities). 4-5 offsuit is too weak to 3-bet for value, and a light raise risks facing a large 3-bet from deep-stacked Elon. I’ll call to see a flop in position, plan to proceed only if I flop something (pair/strong draw) or can take it down cheaply; I’ll fold to significant aggression (a sizable 3-bet or large c-bet I can’t continue against).

Preflop in SB with T4h suited. Excellent pot odds: pot 250 to call 50 (5:1). Multiway pot likely, but suited connector/gapper has good playability, flush and straight potential. Folding from SB to a button limp is too tight. Raising with this hand vs two opponents risks bloating pot OOP with marginal strength. Best to complete cheaply and realize equity on flop.

I have been dealt Ace-King of hearts, a premium starting hand. Both Minni and Elon have limped into the pot. Checking from the big blind with such a strong hand would be a significant mistake. I need to raise to build the pot for value and ideally thin the field. A raise to 500 (5x the big blind) is a standard and effective sizing given the two limpers.

Three-handed on the button with 4♣5♦ facing a raise to 500 after I’d planned to limp — the situation changed. Pot is 700 and it costs 400 to continue, so I’d need roughly 36% equity to break even; 4‑5 offsuit has poor raw equity and poor playability against a raiser’s likely range. 3‑betting as a bluff is possible but too risky given deep stacks and the real chance of a big 4‑bet (Elon is capable of large aggression). Preserve chips and wait for a better spot in position — fold.

Facing a raise to 500 from Flash in the BB after I completed in SB and Minni limped from button (now folded). Pot is 700, calling 400 more gives ~2.2:1 odds (needing ~31% equity). T4h suited has decent playability but is marginal OOP heads-up against a BB isolating raise range, which likely includes pairs 66+, Ax suited/broadway, suited connectors 87s+, making our equity closer to 25-30% and postflop play difficult from SB. No fold equity on a 3bet given deep stacks and opponent's BB position. Better to fold and preserve stack for premium spots in this deep-stack tournament phase.

Q2 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against any reasonable big blind defending range, and no straight or flush potential. Even on the button with position, the pot odds to call 100 into 250 (pot + call) are not sufficient given the hand's trash equity. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in this deep-stacked tournament spot against shorter stacks.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Queen of Spades, a premium starting hand in a 3-handed game. Since Elon folded the button, I am only up against Minni in the Big Blind. I want to raise for value and to take control of the hand. A 3x big blind raise to 300 is a standard and effective sizing here.

Facing a small‑blind open to 300 from 50/100 with T9o in the big blind, this is a straightforward defend. T9o is a playable, connected hand heads‑up and Flash’s SB open is likely fairly wide. I’m getting reasonable pot odds to call (200 more into a 400 pot) and stacks are deep (~70bb), so I can realize equity and play postflop without committing a large portion of my stack. 3‑betting OOP with a marginal hand would bloat the pot and commit chips unnecessarily; folding is too weak given the hand strength and price. I will call the 200 more (300 total committed) and evaluate on the flop.

I have Ace-Queen of Spades on a Jack-high board with one spade. As the pre-flop aggressor, I have the range advantage. While the board has some draws (clubs and straight possibilities), it is generally favorable for my perceived range. I will place a standard continuation bet of 300 (half-pot) to maintain initiative, fold out weak hands, and leverage my overcard equity.

I have top pair (9s) with a T kicker on J-9-2 (two clubs). Flash opened wide from the small blind preflop, so his flop betting range is broad (Js, weaker 9x, overcards, club draws, small pairs, bluffs). He bet a small, 1/3-pot-sized amount (300 into ~900). Pot odds and my hand equity make a call very attractive — I only need ~20% equity to justify a call and I often beat bluffs and many of his non-J hands. Raising OOP with this marginal-but-strong-against-range hand would bloat the pot and can get punished by Js or big turns; folding is too weak. I’ll call to control the pot size, realize equity, and reassess on the turn (I’ll consider raising or folding to large aggression depending on the card and opponent action). Total committed after my call will be 600.

The turn brings a third club (3c), completing potential flushes. As I hold Ace-Queen of Spades, I have no club in my hand and only Ace-high. My flop continuation bet was called, suggesting Minni has a piece of the board or a draw. Given the coordination of the board and being out of position, checking is the best course of action to control the pot and see a free river if possible.

I have top pair (9s) with a T kicker on J‑9‑2‑3 with three clubs on board and no club in my hand. Flash opened wide preflop and bet a small 1/3 pot on the flop, so his range contains lots of weaker pairs, Jx, and club draws — some of which just turned into completed flushes. Checking gives a free river card that can easily beat me; betting charges club draws, gets value from worse pairs and bluff-catchers, and allows me to fold to strong aggression. I'll make a sizing around half the pot to protect and extract value while keeping the pot controllable. If he check‑raises big, I’ll give him credit for a made flush or very strong J and fold; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the river based on the card.

The turn 3c completes the club flush. After I checked, Minni bet 600 into a 1200 pot. I am currently holding Ace-high with no clubs. Given the board texture and the fact that Minni called both pre-flop and on the flop, she likely has a piece of the board (a Jack or a Nine) or a flush. I only have about 13% equity to improve to top pair on the river, and even then, I might already be drawing dead to a flush. Calling without a club blocker or a made hand on this coordinated board is a losing play over the long term. I will fold and preserve my stack.

I am on the button with Ten-Deuce offsuit, which is one of the weakest possible starting hands. While the button offers positional advantage for a steal, the blinds (Elon and Minni) have been relatively active and Elon has a massive stack, making a bluff less likely to succeed without any hand equity. The most prudent move is to fold and wait for a better spot to preserve my stack.

Heads-up vs Elon (BB). Ten-eight hearts is a playable, well-connected suited hand with good postflop playability and I’ll have position after the flop (small blind acts last postflop in heads-up), so I want to seize initiative and build a pot on favourable terms. The pot is 150 and calling would only complete to 100 total; instead I’ll open-raise to 300 total (≈3x the BB). That size gives useful fold equity, discourages too many marginal callers, keeps us well under-stacked vs Elon’s deep stack if he 3-bets, and preserves maneuverability postflop. Plan: c-bet most dry, high-card flops; control pot on very coordinated boards; re-evaluate if Elon 3-bets large (fold or proceed depending on sizing).

Facing a standard 3x raise from the SB after BTN folds, with deep stacks (my 240BB, opp 75BB). Ts9d is a strong defensive hand from the BB vs SB's wide opening range, with good playability, straight potential, and position-independent equity. Pot odds are excellent (calling 200 more into 400, ~2:1 direct + implied). Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP post-flop with a marginal hand if called. Calling allows seeing flop OOP but exploits SB's wide range and my deep stack for post-flop maneuvering. History shows Minni aggressive but check-foldy in spots.

I opened preflop with T8s and was called; on the flop (8d 9s 4c) I have middle pair (pair of 8s) with a decent ten kicker. This is a relatively dry, low-equity board and I'm the preflop aggressor with position — a textbook spot to continuation-bet to get value from worse hands (small pairs, flushless broadways, Kx/Ax floats) and to deny equity to speculative holdings. I size to ~half pot (300 into 600) to extract value while keeping the pot manageable; if Elon calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue to protect/extract, but I’ll fold or strongly temper my action if he check-raises large (which usually represents 9x/overpairs/sets that dominate me).

Facing a 1/3 pot c-bet on this flop (8d9s4c) with top pair (9s) and an open-ended straight draw (8 outs to the nuts), plus backdoor diamond flush draw. My hand Ts9d has excellent equity against Minni's wide SB opening range, which includes many bluffs, weaker pairs, and draws she'll c-bet. Pot odds are favorable even for a call (300 into 1200 ~3:1), but raising builds the pot with a strong made hand + draw OOP, denies equity to her floats/draws, and extracts value from worse pairs/A-high. Her stack (71BB) allows for fold equity if she has air. History shows Minni aggressive pre but folds to post-flop pressure often (e.g., Hand 14,20,23). Raising to 900 (3x her bet) is balanced size for value/protection without overcommitting deep stacks. Calling keeps pot small but allows her to realize equity cheaply; folding is -EV with this monster.

I c-bet a half-pot (300 into 600) and Elon check‑raised big to 900, leaving me 600 to call into a 1,800 pot (~3:1, need >25% equity). My hand is only middle pair (8s) with a ten-kicker and is commonly dominated by the value hands that polarize a large check‑raise here — sets (99/44/88), 9x top-pairs and overpairs (TT+). Elon’s sizing and past aggression point to a value-heavy line, and with deep stacks I’m likely to face further large streets if I continue. Given my earlier plan to fold to big check‑raises and the poor realized equity against his range, I fold and preserve chips for a better spot.

I open-raise to seize position and apply pressure. Q♠T♥ offsuit is a strong button opening hand in a three-handed pot — playable, with decent equity and good positional advantage. A standard 3x BB to 300 gives meaningful fold equity against the blinds while keeping the pot manageable with a marginal holding. Elon (SB) is a very large, aggressive stack and may 3-bet light; I will fold to a substantial 3-bet (e.g. ~3x my open or bigger) but plan to continue against single calls and use position to c-bet favorable flops and extract value where appropriate. With ~71BB effective, this sizing balances aggression and postflop maneuverability.

7d3c is an extremely weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Standard play is to fold the small blind to a button raise, especially out of position against Minni's likely wide but strong enough range. Pot odds are ~1.8:1 (calling 250 into 450 pot), requiring ~35% equity, but 73o realizes far less against a raiser, particularly OOP where post-flop playability is poor. Flash in BB may squeeze or call, increasing multiway risk. Deep stacks don't justify speculative calling; fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

I am in the Big Blind holding King-Nine offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. Given that we are playing 3-handed, a King-high hand with a decent kicker is well within the defending range against a standard button open. The pot odds are favorable, as I need to call 200 to compete for a total pot of 650. While I will be out of position for the rest of the hand, folding would be too tight in this configuration. I will call to see a flop and evaluate based on the board texture.

I have King-high on a board featuring an Ace and a Queen, which heavily favors the pre-flop raiser's range. Since I am out of position and have not connected with the flop in any significant way, checking is the most standard and disciplined play. I will evaluate my next move based on Minni's action, but I am likely looking to fold to any substantial bet given my lack of equity.

Flop: A♦ Q♥ 4♣. I hold Q♠ T♥ — top pair with a decent but non‑nuts kicker. Flash (BB) checked; he called preflop so his range contains lots of A‑x, Q‑x, medium pairs and some weak hands. This is a textbook c‑bet spot: the board is fairly dry, I have value versus worse queens, tens and pocket pairs, and I want to charge A‑high hands and deny free cards. I don’t want to overcommit with a vulnerable one‑pair hand, so I size to about half the pot to get value while keeping the pot manageable if I face resistance. Bet ~350 chips; if he makes a large check‑raise I will strongly consider folding and re‑evaluating, if he calls I’ll continue to a single‑barrel on many turns depending on the card and his tendencies.

I am holding King-high on a flop of Ace-Queen-Four. This board is extremely unfavorable for my hand, as it hits a significant portion of the button's opening range. Minni has continued with a bet of 350 into a 650 pot. Since I have no pair, no draw, and very little potential to improve to the best hand, there is no reason to continue. I will fold and preserve my stack.

3-handed on the button with 84o, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against calling ranges. Even with widened ranges 3-handed, this is below the opening range threshold due to domination risks and bad post-flop prospects. Deep stack but opponents shorter; folding trash preserves stack for better spots. No need to bluff or thin value pre-flop with this.

I am in the Small Blind with J4o, which is a very weak hand. Elon has folded on the button. While it's tempting to try to steal the blind from Minni, J4 offsuit doesn't play well post-flop, especially out of position. Given that Minni has been fairly active, I'll choose the most conservative and disciplined route by folding this hand and waiting for a better opportunity.

I am on the Button with 9d 3c, which is a very weak hand. Even in a 3-handed game, this hand is well below the threshold for an opening range. With Elon as a large stack in the Big Blind and Minni in the Small Blind, the risk of being called or re-raised is high, and the hand has very poor playability post-flop. I will fold and wait for a more advantageous situation.

Heads-up after the button folded — I'm in the small blind with A♠ Q♣, ~75BB effective. This is a strong heads-up hand and I want initiative and fold equity rather than limping/calling out of position. The table's standard open size has been ~3x; I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB) to build the pot, define my range, and force Elon to pay to see flops. If he 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing/read; if he calls I’ll c-bet appropriate flops.

Preflop in BB facing SB raise to 300 after BTN fold. Heads-up vs Minni with 7.25k stack. KJo offsuit is a strong hand against a wide SB stealing range, which Minni employs frequently based on history (e.g., Hands 1,2,6,7,10,16,18,19,22,25,30,32,33,34). Defending wide is standard here; calling gets 3:1 odds but 3-betting adds fold equity (~40-50% vs loose range) while building pot with equity advantage. Postflop, SB acts first but KJo has good playability, blockers to strong hands. Deep stack (24.5k) allows pressure without commitment. Raising to 3x her raise (900 total) is balanced size for value/pressure.

Heads‑up in the SB with A♠Q♣ (~72BB effective). I opened to 300 and Elon 3‑bet to 900. AQs is a strong hand here and I prefer to have initiative rather than playing OOP after calling. Folding is too weak; a flat call surrenders initiative and gives Elon great odds to realize equity. I 4‑bet to 3000 — big enough to fold out small pairs, weaker broadways and many speculative hands, and to extract value/isolate against a wide/aggressive 3‑bettor, but small enough to leave meaningful post‑flop stack depth (~40–45BB) if called. If he calls we proceed with a polarized postflop plan (c‑bet when textures favor us, pot control on dry boards). If he 5‑bets all‑in I’ll re‑evaluate based on shove sizing and his tendencies (he’s aggressive and will have bluffs, so call/fold will be decided then).

Facing a 4-bet to 3000 after my 3-bet to 900 with KJo offsuit. Minni's SB 4-bet range is strong: likely QQ+, AK, possibly JJ/TT. KJo has ~25% equity against that but plays poorly OOP postflop. Pot odds ~2.85:1 require ~26% equity to call, marginal with reverse position and her 4550 behind allowing further pressure. Her history shows aggression but 4-bets indicate value. Folding preserves deep stack (23.7k) for better spots; KJo is a standard fold here vs tight 4-bet range.

4♣7♠ is a weak hand, but in a 3‑handed game the button should open very wide to apply pressure and steal the blinds. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3BB) — a standard-sized open that gives good fold equity vs Flash (BB) and keeps the pot manageable vs Elon (big, aggressive stack). Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet from either opponent; if called I’ll play the flop in position and proceed cautiously, folding to heavy aggression unless I connect. This balances aggression and risk with my 84BB stack.

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). Hand is Jd6c offsuit, a weak speculative hand at best with poor playability OOP postflop. Pot odds are roughly 1.8:1 effective but dominated equity vs typical button raising range. Minni aggressive from history but folding trash preserves stack for premium spots. Deep effective stacks but no reason to defend this wide.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h 5h. Minni opened from the Button to 300. The Small Blind has folded. I need to call 200 chips to see a flop in a pot that will be 650 chips. 85 suited is a reasonable speculative hand to defend the big blind with, especially against a button raise which tends to be wide. My pot odds are roughly 2.25:1, which justifies a call to see if I can flop a strong draw or a piece of the board.

On a paired board like 3s-Td-Th, the pre-flop raiser (Minni) generally has a range advantage, including more combinations of Tens and high pocket pairs. Holding 8h-5h, I have missed this flop entirely and have very little equity beyond very unlikely runner-runner draws. As the Big Blind, the standard play is to check to the aggressor to see how they react. I do not have a strong enough hand to lead out (donk bet) into the button raiser.

I raised preflop and the BB (Flash) just called; the flop is 3♠ T♦ T♥ and I hold 4♣7♠ — no pair and only remote backdoors. In a 3‑handed game my preflop range is very wide, so a continuation bet here can often take the pot away or charge marginal hands that called the big blind. I’ll make a medium c‑bet (300, roughly half the pot) to apply pressure and deny correct odds to many of his calling hands while keeping the pot manageable if I get called. Plan if called: proceed cautiously — I have almost no equity, so I’ll give up to substantial aggression and re‑evaluate on the turn, only barreling again if the turn meaningfully improves my perceived range or the opponent shows clear weakness.

On a paired board like 3s-Td-Th, the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) has a significant range advantage. I have 8-high with no straight or flush draws. While the pot odds are roughly 3:1, I have almost zero equity in this hand and no profitable way to continue. Floating or bluff-raising on such a dry board is low-percentage, so I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket sixes is a premium hand heads-up or 3-handed, especially on the button with position on both opponents. The blinds are relatively deep but play passively based on history (frequent check-calls). Raising preflop takes initiative, builds the pot with a strong hand, and denies equity to weaker hands. Standard raise size of 3x the BB (300 total) balances value and protection. Min raise is 200, but larger size reduces multiway pots.

In a 3-handed game, K8o is a decent hand, but I am in the Small Blind, the worst position at the table. Elon (the Button) has been playing extremely aggressively and has a massive chip lead. Calling out of position with a hand that is easily dominated by Elon's wider range (like K9-AK) is a recipe for losing a large pot. While the price to call is 250 to win a pot of 450, the post-flop disadvantage makes this a fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities where I have position or a stronger hand.

Elon opened the button to 300. I’m in the big blind with TdQc and a healthy 8.7k stack (~87BB). QTo is a defendable hand vs a wide button opening range, and the price is attractive — calling 200 more into a 450 pot gives ~30.8% break-even equity. 3-betting OOP with QTo is too thin against a deep-stacked, often-aggressive opponent (and a min‑3bet to 500 is small and easily called). I’ll call to keep the pot manageable and realize equity out of the blind. Postflop plan: proceed aggressively if I make top pair or a strong draw; otherwise control the pot OOP — fold to heavy pressure on dry boards or call small c-bets with backdoor equity and look for cheap turns to improve.

Flop Qd 3c 2h. I hold QcTd — top pair with a good kicker on an extremely dry board. Pot is 650 and stacks are deep (I have ~8.5k vs Elon ~23.3k). Acting first, checking hands like this OOP just hands initiative to the button and often forces awkward turns; a value bet builds the pot against worse Qx and thin pairs/floats while denying free cards. I’ll make a half‑pot bet (~325). If called I’ll reevaluate on the turn; if I face a large, polarized raise I’ll fold to heavy pressure (likely two pair/set) but may call small raises to continue against bluffs/thin value.

Pocket sixes is an overpair on a dry Q-3-2 board with no flush draws and limited straight draws (mainly 4-5 or A-4). Minni's preflop BB call vs my button raise is wide (Ax, Kx, Qx, suited connectors, pairs). Her flop lead (c-bet into position) is often Qx, mid pairs, or bluffs/blocks. Raising builds the pot for value against weaker Qx (QJ, QT, Q9), Ax, lower pairs, while charging draws and protecting vs overcards. Flat calling risks giving free cards or multi-street decisions. Raise sizing ~3x her bet (to 1000 total) balances value/protection without overbetting deep stacks. Her stack ~8k allows fold equity if behind. History shows she check-calls passively but folds to aggression when beat (e.g., Hand 20, 36).

I have top pair with a decent kicker (QcTd) on a very dry Q♦–3♣–2♥ board. I bet a half‑pot (325) to get value and deny free cards; Elon check‑raised to 1,000, so I must call 675 more (total commitment on the flop = 1,000). The call requires ~25.5% equity (675 / (1975+675)). Elon’s check‑raise is often value‑heavy (sets 33/22, two‑pair Q3/Q2, strong Qx like AQ/KQ) but will also include some bluffs/thin raises given his wide preflop range and deep stack. Raising OOP with a single pair and a medium kicker is too thin and overcommits me; folding here surrenders good pot equity for only 675 chips. I’ll call to realize equity and reevaluate on the turn: fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve, or extract value / go for thin bluffs if the turn is favorable.
